input.prompt:
You are landing Phase 1 spec-sync edits for the ars-ui Pagination component. A drift audit verified the IMPLEMENTATION is correct and the SPEC has two silent-contract bugs plus missing pieces. Your job: edit ONLY `spec/components/navigation/pagination.md` to match the shipped implementation. Do NOT modify any `.rs` file.

Working directory: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/audit-agnostic-navigation-components
Impl (source of truth): crates/ars-components/src/navigation/pagination/mod.rs

Apply these findings (read cited impl lines for exact form):

- P1 (High): §1.5 `on_page_change` (the `PageChange` effect) fires `cb(*ctx.page.get())`. For a CONTROLLED `Bindable`, `set()` writes only the internal value while `get()` returns the unchanged controlled value, so the callback would fire with the OLD page. The impl fires `(callback)(target)` — the requested page (impl ~745-758). Fix the spec §1.5 effect to capture and pass `target`, not `ctx.page.get()`.
- P2 (High): §1.5 attaches the `PageChange` effect only to `GoToPage`; `NextPage`/`PrevPage`/`GoToFirstPage`/`GoToLastPage` use bare `context_only` with no effect, so `on_page_change` never fires for them. The impl routes all five through a `page_change_plan` helper that attaches `PageChange` whenever the page actually changed (impl ~411-429, ~732-743). Update spec §1.5 so every page-mutating event emits `PageChange`, guarded by "page actually changed".
- P3 (Medium): §1.5 `impl Machine` block omits `type Effect = Effect;` though `Effect` is defined — won't compile. Add the associated type (impl ~368).
- P4 (Medium): §1.3 `Context::page_range()` passes `*self.page.get()` (raw, possibly out of range) but §1.1 mandates `current_page()` never expose an out-of-range page. The impl passes `self.current_page()` (clamped) and adds a `Context::current_page()` clamping accessor (impl ~322-331). Fix §1.3 to use a clamped current page and add the `current_page()` helper.
- P5 (Medium): §1.5 `SetPageSize` clamps using `*ctx.page.get()` and emits no effect. The impl clamps using `props.page.unwrap_or(*ctx.page.get())` (preserves the controlled parent's intended page across shrink/expand) and emits `PageChange` only when the rendered page changes (impl ~431-455). Fold this into §1.5 (alongside P2).
- P6 (Medium): §1.5 has no `on_props_changed`; the impl adds one emitting `SyncProps` on page/default_page/page_size/total_items/sibling/boundary change (impl ~494-506). Document it in §1.5.

Conventions:
- Spec Rust code blocks are syntax-parsed by `syn` (`spec compile-snippets`). Keep edited code blocks syntactically valid Rust; partial blocks may use ```rust,no_check (preserve existing fence tags).
- After editing, run `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask spec lint-code`; report results.
- Do NOT touch any `.rs` file. Spec-only.

Report a concise list of edits (section + what changed) and the validate/lint output.

input.prompt:
You are landing Phase 1 spec-sync edits for the ars-ui NavigationMenu component. A drift audit verified the IMPLEMENTATION is correct and reflects the current workspace architecture; the SPEC is stale (predates the typed-Effect/adapter-timer convention used across Popover/Dialog/Tooltip). Your job: edit ONLY `spec/components/navigation/navigation-menu.md` to match the shipped implementation. Do NOT modify any `.rs` file.

Working directory: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/audit-agnostic-navigation-components
Impl (source of truth): crates/ars-components/src/navigation/navigation_menu/mod.rs

Apply these findings (read cited impl lines for exact form):

- N1 (High): §1.7 `Part` enum lists only 8 variants (Root, List, Item, Trigger, Content, Link, Indicator, Viewport); §5.2 prose + the impl + the spec-conformance test all have 15 — the 7 Sub* variants (Sub, SubList, SubItem, SubTrigger, SubContent, SubIndicator, SubViewport). Add the 7 Sub* variants to the spec's §1.7 `Part` enum (impl ~392-464 for exact field shapes, e.g. `SubItem { item_key }`, `SubTrigger { item_key, content_id }`, `SubContent { item_key }`).
- N2 (High): §1.6 effects are inline closures that call `use_platform_effects()`/`set_timeout`/`focus_element_by_id` directly inside `transition`, with string effect names. The impl uses a typed `Effect` enum `{ OpenDelay, CloseDelay, FocusTrigger, ValueChange }` (impl ~119-132) plus `PendingEffect::named(...)`/`cancel_effect(...)`, delegating all DOM/timer work to the adapter; `type Effect = Effect` is declared (~476). Rewrite the §1.6 effect model to the typed-Effect + cancel_effect form and add the `Effect` enum to the spec. Match the impl's transition/effect structure.
- N3 (High): trigger/content DOM ids use raw `item_key.to_string()` in spec (§1.7 ~693; §1.6 focus effects). The impl uses a DOM-safe hashed token via `dom_safe_key_token` (e.g. `nav-trigger-s-<hex>`); `aria-controls`/focus target ids derive the same way. Update §1.6/§1.7 to use the DOM-safe key->id helper.
- N4 (Medium): §1.2 `Event` enum and §1.3 `Context` are missing the registration/sync surface. Add Event variants `SetItems`, `SyncProps`, `SyncControlledValue`, `SyncMessages` (impl ~99-116) and Context fields `items_registered`, `pending_open_item`, `requested_focus_id` (impl ~365-386). Without `SetItems` the spec's focus/motion logic that indexes `ctx.items` can never work.
- N5 (Medium): §1.7 `motion_direction` returns `from-start` on the equal-index/no-previous else branch; the impl returns `None` on tie (`current_index == previous_index`) and from-end/from-start otherwise (impl ~1267-1280). Add the equal-index -> `None` branch to the spec code.
- N6 (Medium): §1.6 opens any key unconditionally; the impl gates open on registered items (`item_is_registered`, `open_item_plan` ~1328-1356) so an unregistered/controlled-stale key can't open a phantom panel. Document the registration gate in §1.6.
- N7 (Medium): §1.7 keydown handlers call `use_platform_effects().now_ms()` internally; the impl takes `now_ms: u64` as a parameter (`on_trigger_keydown(.., now_ms)`, `on_content_keydown(.., now_ms)`, impl ~1213, ~1246). Update the §1.7 handler signatures to accept `now_ms`.

Conventions:
- Spec Rust code blocks are syntax-parsed by `syn` (`spec compile-snippets`). Keep edited code blocks syntactically valid Rust; large illustrative transition/effect blocks may use ```rust,no_check (preserve existing fence tags; this matches existing spec convention for big machine snippets).
- After editing, run `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask spec lint-code`; report results.
- Do NOT touch any `.rs` file. Spec-only.

Report a concise list of edits (section + what changed) and the validate/lint output.

input.prompt:
You are landing Phase 1 spec-sync edits for the ars-ui Link component. A drift audit verified the IMPLEMENTATION is correct and the SPEC is stale. Your job: edit ONLY `spec/components/navigation/link.md` to match the shipped implementation. Do NOT modify any `.rs` file.

Working directory: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/audit-agnostic-navigation-components
Impl (source of truth): crates/ars-components/src/navigation/link/mod.rs

Apply these findings (read cited impl lines for exact form):

- L1 (High): §1.5 `Event::PressEnd` arm is unguarded — `(_, Event::PressEnd)` always transitions to `Focused`. The impl guards it: `Event::PressEnd if ctx.pressed` (impl ~398), so a stray PressEnd while Idle/Focused is a no-op (test `press_end_without_active_press_is_ignored`). Add the `if ctx.pressed` guard to the spec §1.5 arm.
- L2 (Medium): `Event::SyncProps` is declared (spec ~172-174) but has NO transition arm (falls to `_ => None`) and there is NO `on_props_changed`. The impl fully implements both: a `SyncProps` arm re-syncing href/target/rel/is_current/disabled and resetting interaction state when newly disabled (impl ~421-450), plus `on_props_changed` emitting `SyncProps` on any of those field changes (impl ~470-481). Add the `SyncProps` transition arm and `on_props_changed` to §1.5.
- L3 (Medium): the spec machine declares no `type Effect`; the navigate effect is string-named `PendingEffect::new("navigate", …)`. The impl declares `type Effect = Effect` with `enum Effect { Navigate }` (impl ~148-153, ~343) and uses `PendingEffect::new(Effect::Navigate, …)` (~405-407). Add `enum Effect { Navigate }` + `type Effect` to §1.5 and replace the `"navigate"` string.
- L4 (Low): §6.1 `Messages` derive `#[derive(Clone, Debug)]` -> add `PartialEq` (impl line 156; required because `Context: PartialEq`).
- L5 (Low): §1.3 `AriaCurrent` derive `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]` -> add `Copy, Eq, Hash` (impl ~19; impl also has a `const fn as_str()`); and `Target` derive (spec ~42) -> add `Eq, Hash` (impl ~56).

Conventions:
- Spec Rust code blocks are syntax-parsed by `syn` (`spec compile-snippets`). Keep edited code blocks syntactically valid Rust; partial blocks may use ```rust,no_check (preserve existing fence tags).
- After editing, run `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask spec lint-code`; report results.
- Do NOT touch any `.rs` file. Spec-only.

Report a concise list of edits (section + what changed) and the validate/lint output.

- L1 (High): §1.5 `Event::PressEnd` arm is unguarded — `(_, Event::PressEnd)` always transitions to `Focused`. The impl guards it: `Event::PressEnd if ctx.pressed` (impl ~398), so a stray PressEnd while Idle/Focused is a no-op (test `press_end_without_active_press_is_ignored`). - L2 (Medium): `Event::SyncProps` is declared (spec ~172-174) but has NO transition arm (falls to `_ => None`) and there is NO `on_props_changed`. Add the `SyncProps` transition arm and `on_props_changed` to §1.5.
- L3 (Medium): the spec machine declares no `type Effect`; the navigate effect is string-named `PendingEffect::new("navigate", …)`. The impl declares `type Effect = Effect` with `enum Effect { Navigate }` (impl ~148-153, ~343) and uses `PendingEffect::new(Effect::Navigate, …)` (~405-407). input.prompt:
You are landing Phase 1 spec-sync edits for the ars-ui Steps component. A drift audit verified the IMPLEMENTATION is correct and the SPEC is missing several controlled-mode and prop-reactivity contracts. Your job: edit ONLY `spec/components/navigation/steps.md` to match the shipped implementation. Do NOT modify any `.rs` file.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT change the §1.5 `NextStep` last-step arm — the spec already marks the final step `Status::Complete` on completion, which is the intended behavior (the implementation is being changed separately to match the spec here, so leave the spec's last-step NextStep code as-is).

Working directory: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/audit-agnostic-navigation-components
Impl (source of truth): crates/ars-components/src/navigation/steps/mod.rs

Apply these findings (read cited impl lines for exact form):

- S2 (High): §1.5 step-navigation transitions (`GoToStep`/`NextStep`/`PrevStep`) unconditionally mutate `ctx.statuses` + `ctx.step.set(target)` with no controlled/uncontrolled split. The impl factors a `step_change_plan` helper that, when `step` is controlled, skips ALL context mutation and only fires `Effect::StepChange` with the requested target (impl ~893-899). Document the controlled/uncontrolled split in §1.5 (controlled trees emit only `Effect::StepChange`; the spec currently never mentions controlled-mode echo for step navigation).
- S3 (Medium): §1.5 `SetStatus { status: Current }` has no controlled guard. The impl rejects `SetStatus{Current}` entirely when `step.is_controlled()` (`return None`, impl ~482-484). Add the controlled guard to the spec arm.
- S4 (Medium): §1.5 `CompleteStep(idx)` and `SetStatus{step}` do no explicit bounds check. The impl adds `if step >= ctx.count.get() { return None; }` (impl ~468, ~482) so out-of-range events produce no transition. Add the `>= count` early-return to both spec arms.
- S5 (Medium): §1.3 `Context` has no `statuses_prop` field. The impl adds `Context.statuses_prop: Option<Vec<Status>>` (impl ~295-296) and uses it in `SyncProps` to PRESERVE runtime status progress when the `statuses` prop is unchanged across an unrelated prop change (impl ~507-511). Add `statuses_prop` to §1.3 `Context` and the preserve-vs-reset logic to §1.5 `SyncProps`.
- S6 (Medium): §1.5 has `SyncProps` but no `on_props_changed`. The impl adds `on_props_changed` gating `SyncProps` on step/default_step/count/statuses/linear/orientation changes (impl ~542-554). Add `on_props_changed` to §1.5.
- S7 (Low but important — spec self-contradiction): §1.5 `next_trigger_attrs` disables Next at the last step AND `on_next_trigger_click` guards `if !is_last_step`, which together make completion-via-the-Next-button impossible — contradicting the spec's own `Effect::Complete`/`on_complete` design. The impl keeps Next ENABLED at the last step (`next_trigger_attrs` never disables) and `on_next_trigger_click` always sends `NextStep` (impl ~770-773, ~783-785). Fix §1.5: `next_trigger_attrs` must NOT disable at last step, and `on_next_trigger_click` must NOT guard `is_last_step`. Update the §2 anatomy note about NextTrigger accordingly.
- S8 (Low): §1.3 `Status` derive -> add `Eq, Hash` (impl ~35); §4 `Messages` derive `#[derive(Clone, Debug)]` -> add `PartialEq` (impl ~105, required by `Context: PartialEq`).
- S9 (Low): §4 — optionally introduce a `StepLabelFn` type alias (impl ~102 extracts `pub type StepLabelFn`). Cosmetic; add it if it reads cleanly.

Conventions:
- Spec Rust code blocks are syntax-parsed by `syn` (`spec compile-snippets`). Keep edited code blocks syntactically valid Rust; partial blocks may use ```rust,no_check (preserve existing fence tags).
- After editing, run `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask spec lint-code`; report results.
- Do NOT touch any `.rs` file. Spec-only.

Report a concise list of edits (section + what changed) and the validate/lint output.

## Summary

All six findings applied to `spec/components/navigation/pagination.md` only. No `.rs` file touched.

### Edits

**§1.3 Context (P4 — out-of-range page)**
- Added `Context::current_page()` clamping accessor (`clamp_page(*self.page.get(), self.page_count)`), with a doc comment explaining controlled-`Bindable` staleness and tying it to the §1.1 mandate.
- Changed `page_range()` to pass `self.current_page()` instead of the raw `*self.page.get()`.

**§1.5 Full Machine Implementation**
- **P3:** Added `type Effect = Effect;` to the `impl ars_core::Machine` block (was omitted despite `Effect` being defined — wouldn't compile).
- **P1 + P2:** Rewrote `transition` so all five page-mutating events (`GoToPage`, `NextPage`, `PrevPage`, `GoToFirstPage`, `GoToLastPage`) route through a new `page_change_plan` helper that attaches the `PageChange` effect only when the clamped target differs from the current page. Added `with_page_change_effect`, whose effect fires `(callback)(target)` — the requested page — instead of `cb(*ctx.page.get())`, fixing the stale-value bug for controlled bindings. Navigation now computes deltas from `ctx.current_page()` (clamped), not raw `page.get()`.
- **P5:** `SetPageSize` now clamps `props.page.unwrap_or(*ctx.page.get())` (preserving a controlled parent's intended page across shrink/expand) and emits `PageChange` only when the rendered page actually changes (`emit = target != current`).
- **P6:** Added `on_props_changed` emitting `Event::SyncProps` when any of `page`, `default_page`, `page_size`, `total_items`, `sibling_count`, or `boundary_count` changes.
- Added the `clamp_page` free function used by the above.
- `SyncProps` now uses `clamp_page(...)` for its controlled/target computation (consistent with the new helper) rather than inline `.max(1).min(...)`.

Fence tags preserved: §1.3 and §1.5 remain plain ```rust (syntax-only `syn` parsing); all additions are syntactically valid Rust.

### Validation
- `cargo xtask spec validate` → `Validated 340 files. All checks passed.`

Total finding count is identical (202) with and without my edits, confirming my change introduces no new lint findings. The 202 pre-existing findings are all in unrelated utility files (heading, highlight, keyboard, landmark, etc.), out of scope.

Note: the worktree already contained other uncommitted changes (test reorg `.rs` files and four other navigation `.md` files) before this task. I did not touch any of them; my only modification is `spec/components/navigation/pagination.md` (+103/-40).</result>

content.text: Now the big piece — TreeView §5/§6 variants. Let me read the spec sections fully first.

content.content:
1670	
1671	// `DropPosition` and `CollectionDropTarget` — defined in `ars_collections::dnd`
1672	// (`06-collections.md` §10). Reconciliation: `CollectionDndEvent::Reorder`/`Move`
1673	// is the low-level *collection* contract (flat `keys` + a single `target`);
1674	// `ReorderEvent` is the TreeView *component* surface (hierarchical root→node
1675	// paths). The adapter maps between them.
1676	```
1677	
1678	## 5. Variant: Lazy Loading
1679	
1680	`TreeView` supports lazy loading of child nodes for large or server-driven hierarchies. When a node is first expanded and its children are not yet loaded, the machine fires a `LoadChildren` event.
1681	
1682	### 5.1 Additional Props
1683	
1684	```rust,no_check
1685	/// The load_children callback is registered in the adapter layer, not in Props.
1686	/// When provided, nodes without pre-loaded children will trigger lazy loading on expand.
1687	/// The adapter observes `LoadChildren(Key)` events and calls the user-provided callback.
1688	```
1689	
1690	```rust
1691	/// Type alias for the lazy-load callback.
1692	/// The adapter calls this with the parent node's key when children need loading.
1693	/// The callback should eventually send a `ChildrenLoaded` event back to the machine.
1694	/// Uses `Rc` so the adapter can clone it into event handler closures.
1695	pub type LoadChildrenFn = Rc<dyn Fn(Key)>;
1696	```
1697	
1698	### 5.2 Additional Events
1699	
1700	```rust,no_check
1701	/// Added to TreeView Event enum.
1702	/// Fired when a node is expanded for the first time and has no children loaded.
1703	LoadChildren(Key),
1704	/// Fired by the consumer (via adapter callback) when lazy-loaded children arrive.
1705	ChildrenLoaded {
1706	    /// The parent node key.
1707	    parent: Key,
1708	    /// The loaded children nodes.
1709	    children: Vec<Node<T>>,
1710	},
1711	```
1712	
1713	### 5.3 Additional Node State
1714	
1715	```rust
1716	/// Extended NodeState to include loading.
1717	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
1718	pub enum NodeLoadState {
1719	    /// Children are present in the collection.
1720	    Loaded,
1721	    /// Children are being fetched (loading indicator shown).
1722	    Loading,
1723	    /// Load failed — adapter may show retry affordance.
1724	    Error,
1725	    /// Children have not been requested yet (initial state for lazy nodes).
1726	    NotLoaded,
1727	}
1728	```
1729	
1730	The `tree_view::Context` tracks `load_state: BTreeMap<Key, NodeLoadState>`.
1731	
1732	### 5.4 Behavior
1733	
1734	1. User expands a node whose `load_state` is `NotLoaded`.
1735	2. Machine transitions `load_state` to `Loading` and fires `LoadChildren(key)`.
1736	3. Adapter calls `load_children(key)` prop. The consumer fetches data asynchronously.
1737	4. Consumer sends `ChildrenLoaded { parent, children }` when data arrives.
1738	5. Machine inserts children into the collection and transitions `load_state` to `Loaded`.
1739	6. If loading fails, consumer sends `LoadError(key)` and machine transitions to `Error`.
1740	
1741	## 6. Variant: Renamable Nodes
1742	
1743	`TreeView` supports inline rename of tree nodes, similar to file-explorer rename behavior.
1744	When enabled, users can start renaming a node via F2 or slow double-click. The node's text
1745	display is replaced by an inline `<input>` element until the rename is committed or cancelled.
1746	
1747	### 6.1 Additional Props
1748	
1749	```rust,no_check
1750	/// Additional props for the renamable variant.
1751	impl Props {
1752	    /// When true, tree items can be renamed inline via F2 or slow double-click.
1753	    /// Default: `false`.
1754	    pub renamable: bool,
1755	}
1756	
1757	// In Props::default():
1758	// renamable: false,
1759	```
1760	
1761	### 6.2 Additional Events
1762	
1763	```rust,no_check
1764	/// Added to TreeView Event enum for the renamable variant.
1765	
1766	/// User initiates rename on a node (e.g., F2 or slow double-click).
1767	RenameStart(Key),
1768	/// User commits the new name.
1769	RenameCommit { key: Key, new_name: String },
1770	/// User cancels rename (Escape or blur without change).
1771	RenameCancel(Key),
1772	```
1773	
1774	### 6.3 Additional Context
1775	
1776	```rust,no_check
1777	/// Added to TreeView Context for the renamable variant.
1778	impl Context {
1779	    /// The node currently being renamed, if any. When `Some(key)`, the node
1780	    /// identified by `key` renders a `NodeRenameInput` instead of its text label.
1781	    pub renaming_key: Option<Key>,
1782	}
1783	
1784	// In Machine::init():
1785	// renaming_key: None,
1786	```
1787	
1788	### 6.4 Behavior
1789	
1790	- **`RenameStart(key)`**: Only allowed from `Idle` or `Focused` states. If `renamable` is
1791	  false or the target node is disabled, the event is ignored. If another node is currently
1792	  being renamed (`renaming_key` is `Some(other_key)`), that rename is committed first (fires
1793	  `RenameCommit` for the previous node with its current input value), then `renaming_key` is
1794	  set to `Some(key)`. Focus moves to the rename input.
1795	- **`RenameCommit { key, new_name }`**: Clears `renaming_key` to `None`. The consumer is
1796	  responsible for persisting the new name (e.g., updating the `TreeCollection` data source).
1797	  The machine does not modify the `TreeItem.label` — the consumer updates the collection and
1798	  passes new props.
1799	- **`RenameCancel(key)`**: Clears `renaming_key` to `None`. No value change occurs. Focus
1800	  returns to the tree item that was being renamed.
1801	
1802	```rust,no_check
1803	/// Transition logic for rename events.
1804	/// Added to the Machine::transition match block.
1805	
1806	Event::RenameStart(key) => {
1807	    if !_props.renamable { return None; }
1808	    let node_disabled = ctx.items.get(key)
1809	        .and_then(|n| n.value.as_ref())
1810	        .map(|v| v.disabled)
1811	        .unwrap_or(false);
1812	    if node_disabled { return None; }
1813	    match state {
1814	        State::Idle | State::Focused => {
1815	            let key = key.clone();
1816	            Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Focused).apply(move |ctx| {
1817	                ctx.renaming_key = Some(key.clone());
1818	                ctx.focused_node = Some(key);
1819	            }))
1820	        }
1821	        _ => None,
1822	    }
1823	}
1824	
1825	Event::RenameCommit { key, .. } => {
1826	    if ctx.renaming_key.as_ref() != Some(key) { return None; }
1827	    Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(|ctx| {
1828	        ctx.renaming_key = None;
1829	    }))
1830	}
1831	
1832	Event::RenameCancel(key) => {
1833	    if ctx.renaming_key.as_ref() != Some(key) { return None; }
1834	    Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(|ctx| {
1835	        ctx.renaming_key = None;
1836	    }))
1837	}
1838	```
1839	
1840	### 6.5 Anatomy Additions
1841	
1842	```text
1843	TreeView (renamable variant)
1844	└── Root
1845	    ├── Branch
1846	    │   ├── BranchControl
1847	    │   │   ├── BranchIndicator
1848	    │   │   ├── BranchText           (hidden when renaming_key matches)
1849	    │   │   └── NodeRenameInput(key)   (shown when renaming_key matches)
1850	    │   └── BranchContent
1851	    └── Leaf
1852	        ├── LeafText                 (hidden when renaming_key matches)
1853	        └── NodeRenameInput(key)       (shown when renaming_key matches)
1854	```
1855	
1856	The `NodeRenameInput(Key)` part is an `<input type="text">` element rendered inside the
1857	branch control or leaf when `renaming_key == Some(key)`. It replaces the text display during
1858	rename and is pre-filled with the node's current `label` value.
1859	
1860	```rust,no_check
1861	/// Added to Part enum (node identity is `Key`, consistent with the core).
1862	NodeRenameInput { node_id: Key },
1863	```
1864	
1865	```rust
1866	/// Attrs for the inline rename input.
1867	/// Rendered only when `renaming_key == Some(key)` for this node.
1868	pub fn node_rename_input_attrs(&self, node_id: &Key) -> AttrMap {
1869	    let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1870	    let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] =
1871	        Part::NodeRenameInput { node_id: Key::default() }.data_attrs();
1872	    attrs.set(scope_attr, scope_val);
1873	    attrs.set(part_attr, part_val);
1874	    let label = self.get_node(node_id)
1875	        .and_then(|n| n.value.as_ref())
1876	        .map(|v| v.label.as_str())
1877	        .unwrap_or("");
1878	    attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Type, "text");
1879	    attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Value, label);
1880	    attrs.set(
1881	        HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Label),
1882	        (self.ctx.messages.rename_label)(label, &self.ctx.locale),
1883	    );
1884	    attrs
1885	}
1886	
1887	/// Handle keydown events on the rename input.
1888	pub fn on_rename_input_keydown(&self, node_id: &Key, key_code: &str, current_value: &str) {
1889	    match key_code {
1890	        "Enter" => {
1891	            (self.send)(Event::RenameCommit {
1892	                key: node_id.clone(),
1893	                new_name: current_value.to_string(),
1894	            });
1895	        }
1896	        "Escape" => {
1897	            (self.send)(Event::RenameCancel(node_id.clone()));
1898	        }
1899	        _ => {}
1900	    }
1901	}
1902	
1903	/// Handle blur on the rename input — commits the rename if still active.
1904	pub fn on_rename_input_blur(&self, node_id: &Key, current_value: &str) {
1905	    if self.ctx.renaming_key.as_ref() == Some(node_id) {
1906	        (self.send)(Event::RenameCommit {
1907	            key: node_id.clone(),
1908	            new_name: current_value.to_string(),
1909	        });
1910	    }
1911	}
1912	
1913	/// Check if a node is currently being renamed.
1914	pub fn is_renaming(&self, node_id: &Key) -> bool {
1915	    self.ctx.renaming_key.as_ref() == Some(node_id)
1916	}
1917	```
1918	
1919	The `part_attrs` match is extended:
1920	
1921	```rust,no_check
1922	Part::NodeRenameInput { ref node_id } => self.node_rename_input_attrs(node_id),
1923	```
1924	
1925	### 6.6 Accessibility
1926	
1927	| Key      | Behavior                                                       |
1928	| -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
1929	| `F2`     | Start rename on the focused node (when `renamable` is true).   |
1930	| `Enter`  | Commit the rename (when rename input is focused).              |
1931	| `Escape` | Cancel the rename, discard changes, return focus to tree item. |
1932	
1933	- The `NodeRenameInput` receives `aria-label` from `Messages.rename_label`, which receives
1934	  the node's current label as the first argument. Screen readers announce e.g.,
1935	  "Rename Documents" when focus enters the input.
1936	- When rename starts, focus moves to the `NodeRenameInput`. When rename ends (commit or
1937	  cancel), focus returns to the tree item.
1938	- The rename input is not part of the tab sequence (`tabindex="-1"` is not needed because
1939	  it is a native `<input>` that receives focus programmatically).
1940	
1941	### 6.7 Messages
1942	
1943	```rust,no_check
1944	/// Added to the Messages struct for the renamable variant.
1945	impl Messages {
1946	    /// Accessible label for the rename input. Called with the node's current
1947	    /// label and the resolved locale.
1948	    /// Default: `|node_name, _locale| format!("Rename {}", node_name)`
1949	    pub rename_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1950	}
1951	
1952	// In Messages::default():
1953	// rename_label: MessageFn::new(|node_name, _locale| format!("Rename {}", node_name)),
1954	```
1955	
1956	## 7. Library Parity
1957	
1958	> Compared against: Ark UI (`TreeView`), React Aria (`Tree`).
1959	
1960	### 7.1 Props
1961	
1962	| Feature             | ars-ui                             | Ark UI                                 | React Aria                     | Notes                                       |
1963	| ------------------- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
1964	| Tree data source    | `items: TreeCollection<TreeItem>`  | `collection: TreeCollection<T>`        | `items: Iterable<T>`           | All provide collection-based API            |
1965	| Controlled selected | `selected`                         | --                                     | `selectedKeys`                 | Ark uses context API                        |
1966	| Default selected    | `default_selected`                 | `defaultSelectedValue`                 | `defaultSelectedKeys`          | Full match                                  |
1967	| Controlled expanded | `expanded`                         | `expandedValue`                        | `expandedKeys`                 | Full match                                  |
1968	| Default expanded    | `default_expanded`                 | `defaultExpandedValue`                 | `defaultExpandedKeys`          | Full match                                  |
1969	| Selection mode      | `selection_mode`                   | `selectionMode`                        | `selectionMode`                | Full match                                  |

## Summary of edits to `spec/components/navigation/accordion.md`

All changes are spec-only; no `.rs` file was touched. The spec's §1.5 machine was a stale stub; it now matches `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/accordion/mod.rs`.

**A1 (High) — §1.5 `transition`:** Rewrote the whole function. It now has a dedicated arm for every event with no `_ => None` fall-through. `SetItems`, `SyncProps`, `SyncControlledValue`, and `SetDirection` are fully implemented by dispatching to `set_items_plan`, `sync_props_plan`, `sync_controlled_value_plan`, and an inline `SetDirection` arm (no-op when `dir` unchanged), mirroring impl ~422-432/456-460.

**A2 (High) — focus movement:** Replaced the single collapsed arm (which called the phantom `next_enabled_item`) with four distinct arms (`FocusNext`/`FocusPrev`/`FocusFirst`/`FocusLast`) using the anchor+step model. Added the helper free functions `focus_anchor`, `step_focus`, `enabled_items`, `focus_item_transition`, plus the `FocusStep` enum, matching impl ~434-454/983-1027.

**A3 (High) — `on_props_changed`:** Added to the `Machine` impl, emitting `SetDirection`/`SyncProps`/`SyncControlledValue` with the controlled→uncontrolled exit branch (`else if !props_changed { SyncProps }`), matching impl ~478-504.

40	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
41	
42	use super::key_token::dom_safe_key_token;
43	
44	/// Value type stored in the [`TreeCollection`] backing a [`TreeView`](self).
45	///
46	/// Carries the per-node properties the machine needs that are not part of the
47	/// collection's structural model: the display label, disabled state, the
48	/// lazy-load `has_children` affordance, and an optional navigation `href`.
49	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
50	pub struct TreeItem {
51	    /// Display label for the node (used in typeahead and accessibility).
52	    pub label: String,
53	
54	    /// Whether this node is disabled. Disabled nodes are not selectable and
55	    /// cannot be dragged; they remain focusable so their state is announced.
56	    pub disabled: bool,
57	
58	    /// When `true`, the node renders an expand affordance even if it has no
59	    /// children loaded yet. Essential for lazy-loaded trees where the child
60	    /// count is unknown until the node is first expanded.
61	    pub has_children: bool,
62	
63	    /// Optional navigation URL. When `Some`, the clickable area renders as an
64	    /// `<a>` element (native middle-click / Ctrl+click), while the parent
65	    /// `role="treeitem"` is preserved.
66	    pub href: Option<String>,
67	}
68	
69	/// Machine states for [`TreeView`](self).
70	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
71	pub enum State {
72	    /// The tree does not have keyboard focus.
73	    #[default]
74	    Idle,
75	
76	    /// The tree has keyboard focus on some node.
77	    Focused,
78	}
79	
80	/// Events accepted by the [`TreeView`](self) state machine.
81	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
82	pub enum Event {
83	    /// Expand a branch node.
84	    ExpandNode(Key),
85	
86	    /// Collapse a branch node.
87	    CollapseNode(Key),
88	
89	    /// Toggle the expand/collapse state of a branch node.
90	    ToggleNode(Key),
91	
92	    /// Add a node to the selection (respecting selection mode/behavior).
93	    SelectNode(Key),
94	
95	    /// Remove a node from the selection.
96	    DeselectNode(Key),
97	
98	    /// Move the focus indicator to a specific node.
99	    FocusNode(Key),
100	
101	    /// Move focus to the next visible node (wraps to the first).
102	    FocusNext,
103	
104	    /// Move focus to the previous visible node (wraps to the last).
105	    FocusPrev,
106	
107	    /// Move focus to the first visible node.
108	    FocusFirst,
109	
110	    /// Move focus to the last visible node.
111	    FocusLast,
112	
113	    /// Move focus to the parent of the focused node.
114	    FocusParent,
115	
116	    /// The tree container received focus.
117	    Focus {
118	        /// Whether focus arrived via the keyboard.
119	        is_keyboard: bool,
120	    },
121	
122	    /// The tree container lost focus.
123	    Blur,
124	
125	    /// Append a character to the typeahead buffer and jump to the next matching
126	    /// node. Carries the current time in milliseconds (adapter-provided) so the
127	    /// shared [`typeahead::State`] can reset the buffer after its timeout.
128	    TypeaheadSearch(char, u64),
129	
130	    /// Reset the typeahead buffer (e.g. on blur or an explicit timeout tick).
131	    ClearTypeahead,
132	
133	    /// Expand every expandable node in the tree.
134	    ExpandAll,
135	
136	    /// Collapse every node in the tree.
137	    CollapseAll,
138	
139	    /// Begin a drag (keyboard pickup or pointer drag start) on a node.
140	    /// Ignored unless [`Props::dnd_enabled`].
141	    DragStart(Key),
142	
143	    /// Set the resolved drop target (adapter pointer hit-testing). Ignored when
144	    /// no drag is active or the target is invalid.
145	    DragOver(CollectionDropTarget),
146	
147	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the next valid slot.
148	    DragMoveNext,
149	
150	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the previous valid slot.
151	    DragMovePrev,
152	
153	    /// Confirm the drop at the current drop target (fires [`Props::on_reorder`]).
154	    Drop,
155	
156	    /// Cancel the active drag and discard the drop target.
157	    CancelDrag,
158	
159	    /// Re-sync prop-derived context (the data source and controlled
160	    /// `selected`/`expanded` bindings) after the consumer supplies new props.
161	    /// Emitted by [`Machine::on_props_changed`](ars_core::Machine::on_props_changed).
162	    SyncProps,
163	}
164	
165	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the [`TreeView`](self) machine.
166	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
167	pub enum Effect {
168	    /// Adapter must scroll the active-descendant node ([`Context::focused_node`])
169	    /// into view. The agnostic core never moves DOM focus itself; the tree uses
170	    /// the active-descendant pattern, so only scrolling is adapter-resolved.
171	    ScrollFocusedIntoView,
172	
173	    /// Adapter invokes [`Props::on_selection_change`] with the requested
174	    /// selection set. Emitted on every user-requested selection change so a
175	    /// controlled parent can echo the new value back through `Props::selected`.
176	    SelectionChange,
177	
178	    /// Adapter invokes [`Props::on_expanded_change`] with the requested expanded
179	    /// set. Emitted on every user-requested expansion change so a controlled
180	    /// parent can echo the new value back through `Props::expanded`.
181	    ExpandedChange,
182	
183	    /// Adapter invokes [`Props::on_reorder`] with the completed [`ReorderEvent`].
184	    Reorder,
185	}
186	
187	/// Emitted when a tree node is reordered via drag-and-drop.
188	///
189	/// Paths are sequences of keys from the root to the item, so consumers can
190	/// apply the reorder against their hierarchical data source without resolving
191	/// flat indices.
192	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
193	pub struct ReorderEvent {
194	    /// Path of the dragged node (root → node).
195	    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
196	
197	    /// Path of the drop target node (root → target).
198	    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
199	
200	    /// Where, relative to the target, the node was dropped.
201	    pub position: DropPosition,
202	}
203	
204	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::loading_label`].
205	pub type LoadingLabelFn = dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
206	
207	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::drag_handle_label`]. Receives the
208	/// node's label and the active locale.
209	pub type DragHandleLabelFn = dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
210	
211	/// Localizable messages for [`TreeView`](self).
212	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
213	pub struct Messages {
214	    /// Loading indicator text for branches whose children are being fetched
215	    /// (default: `"Loading…"`). Rendered as visually hidden text and announced
216	    /// via `aria-live="polite"` by adapters.
217	    pub loading_label: MessageFn<LoadingLabelFn>,
218	
219	    /// Accessible label template for a node's drag handle, called with the
220	    /// node's label (default: `"Drag {label}"`).
221	    pub drag_handle_label: MessageFn<DragHandleLabelFn>,
222	}
223	
224	impl Default for Messages {
225	    fn default() -> Self {
226	        Self {
227	            loading_label: MessageFn::static_str("Loading\u{2026}"),
228	            drag_handle_label: MessageFn::new(|label: &str, _locale: &Locale| {
229	                alloc::format!("Drag {label}")
230	            }),
231	        }
232	    }
233	}
234	
235	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
236	
237	/// Immutable configuration for a [`TreeView`](self) instance.
238	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, ars_core::HasId)]
239	pub struct Props {
240	    /// Component instance id (base for generated part/node ids).
241	    pub id: String,
242	
243	    /// The tree data source.
244	    pub items: TreeCollection<TreeItem>,
245	
246	    /// Controlled selected node keys. `Some` = controlled, `None` = uncontrolled.
247	    pub selected: Option<selection::Set>,
248	
249	    /// Uncontrolled initial selection.
250	    pub default_selected: selection::Set,
251	
252	    /// Controlled expanded branch keys. `Some` = controlled, `None` = uncontrolled.
253	    pub expanded: Option<BTreeSet<Key>>,
254	
255	    /// Default expanded branch keys when uncontrolled.
256	    pub default_expanded: BTreeSet<Key>,
257	
258	    /// Allow multiple nodes to be selected simultaneously (drives
259	    /// `aria-multiselectable`).
260	    pub multiple: bool,
261	
262	    /// Which nodes can be selected.
263	    pub selection_mode: selection::Mode,
264	
265	    /// Selection behavior: `Toggle` (checkbox-like) or `Replace`
266	    /// (file-explorer-like).
267	    pub selection_behavior: selection::Behavior,
268	
269	    /// Enable the drag-and-drop reorder surface.

- **§1.5 SetStatus (S3, S4)** — Added combined guard `if step_index >= count || (status == Current && ctx.step.is_controlled()) { return None; }`, and reordered the `Current` branch to match the impl (set slot, then `step.set`, then normalize; else-branch for non-`Current`).
- **§1.5 SyncProps (S5)** — Added the preserve-vs-reset logic: when `statuses_prop == ctx.statuses_prop`, re-normalize existing runtime statuses; otherwise reset to the new prop. Writes `ctx.statuses_prop = statuses_prop` in the closure.
- **§1.5 step_change_plan helper (S2)** — Added a new module-level `fn step_change_plan(...) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Machine>>` documenting the controlled/uncontrolled split: uncontrolled mutates context + fires `Effect::StepChange`; controlled performs no context mutation (`if controlled { return; }`) and fires only `Effect::StepChange` with the requested `target`. ## Summary

All six findings (T1–T6) applied to `spec/components/navigation/tabs.md` only. No implementation or test `.rs` files were modified.

### Edits

- **T5 — §1.5 `State` derive**: Added `Default, Eq` to the derive and `#[default]` on `Idle` → `#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]` with `#[default] Idle` (matches impl line 61).

- **T1 + T6 — §1.5 `Event` enum**: Dropped `Eq` from the derive → `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]`, with a doc note that `SyncMessages` carries `Messages` (only `PartialEq` via `Arc::ptr_eq`). Added the missing variants `SyncMessages { locale: Locale, messages: Messages }` (impl ~159-165) and `SyncControlledValue(Option<Key>)` (impl ~203). Also added `SyncProps` — the spec enum was likewise missing it even though the spec's own §1.5 transition match arm (`Event::SyncProps`) and the §1.2 table reference it, so adding it was required to actually make the enum exhaustive vs its own match (the stated T1 goal).

- **T1 follow-through — §1.5 transition match**: Added a `SyncMessages` match arm (mirroring impl ~1000-1014, idempotent locale/messages update) so the newly-added enum variant is not orphaned and the match stays exhaustive.

- **T2 — §1.6 + §3.3 selection accessors**: Updated `selected_tab` to `.filter(|key| !is_disabled(self.ctx, key))` and `is_tab_selected` to `&& !is_disabled(self.ctx, tab_key)` (impl ~1441-1453). Added doc-comment notes on both methods, a "Disabled-selection semantics" prose paragraph at the end of §1.6, and a new §3.3 bullet — all stating the deliberate asymmetry: the filter excludes *disabled* keys but **not** *unregistered* keys (an unregistered controlled value still reports as selected).

- **T3 — §1.6 `is_tablist_focus_fallback`**: Replaced the `value.is_some()` early-bail with the impl's registered-selection check (`any_registered_tab_is_selected`, impl ~1637-1650), and rewrote the doc comment to cover both the `value == None` and the controlled-stale-unregistered-key cases, explicitly noting the guard keys off whether any *registered* tab is selected rather than `value.is_some()`.

- **T4 — §1.6 `tab_attrs` / `panel_attrs` code + docs**: Changed all four `ids.item("tab"/"panel", tab_key)` call sites to `ids.item(..., &dom_safe_key_token(tab_key))` (matching impl `tab_dom_id`/`panel_dom_id` ~1946-1952) and updated the doc comments to reference the DOM-safe token (cross-linking the §2 prose that already mandates it).

### Validation

- `cargo xtask spec validate` — `Validated 340 files. All checks passed.`
- `cargo xtask spec lint-code` — **zero findings for `navigation/tabs.md`** (the findings it prints are all pre-existing in unrelated files: `utility/heading.md`, `highlight.md`, `keyboard.md`, `landmark.md`, etc., none of which I touched).
- `cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets` — `1 passed` (all tabs Rust blocks still parse under `syn`).

All ten Rust blocks in the file are plain ```rust fences (syn-parsed); the new `Locale`/`Messages` type references and `dom_safe_key_token(...)` call sites are syntactically valid and confirmed by the passing snippet test.</result>

content.content:
120	// Effect identity types
121	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	
123	/// Marker supertrait that names the bounds every [`Machine::Effect`] type
124	/// must satisfy.
125	///
126	/// `EffectName` carries no methods — it is a pure naming alias for the
127	/// supertrait sum so call sites can write `type Effect: EffectName`
128	/// instead of repeating
129	/// `Copy + Debug + Eq + Hash + Send + Sync + 'static` everywhere. The
130	/// blanket impl below means **any** type that already meets those
131	/// bounds (bespoke component enums, [`NoEffect`], `&'static str`, …)
132	/// satisfies `EffectName` automatically; there is no opt-in step.
133	///
134	/// The bounds themselves come from how adapters use effect names:
135	///
136	/// - `Copy` — adapters drain `cancel_effects: Vec<M::Effect>` without
137	///   cloning each entry; `Copy` makes that ergonomic.
138	/// - `Debug` — `SendResult`/`TransitionPlan` log effect names directly
139	///   via `Debug`, and devtools panels render the `Debug` output.
140	/// - `Eq + Hash` — adapters store cleanup closures in a
141	///   `HashMap<M::Effect, CleanupFn>`, keyed by the typed effect name.
142	/// - `Send + Sync + 'static` — `Service<M>` is held inside framework
143	///   reactivity (Leptos `StoredValue`, Dioxus `Signal`) which require
144	///   thread-safety on the value.
145	///
146	/// This follows the same marker-supertrait idiom the crate uses for
147	/// [`BindableValue`].
148	pub trait EffectName: Copy + Debug + Eq + core::hash::Hash + Send + Sync + 'static {}
149	
150	impl<T: Copy + Debug + Eq + core::hash::Hash + Send + Sync + 'static> EffectName for T {}
151	
152	/// An uninhabited effect type for machines that never emit named effects.
153	///
154	/// Use as `type Effect = NoEffect` on machines whose `transition`
155	/// implementations always return effect-free [`TransitionPlan`]s. Because
156	/// the type is empty, `PendingEffect::<_>::named(...)` is uncallable —
157	/// the type system enforces "this machine has no intents", which is
158	/// stronger than `type Effect = ()` (which would let you accidentally
159	/// emit `PendingEffect::named(())`).
160	///
161	/// Machines that emit ad-hoc string-named effects during prototyping
162	/// (or in internal test fixtures) can use `type Effect = &'static str`
163	/// directly — `&'static str` already implements [`EffectName`].
164	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
165	pub enum NoEffect {}
166	
167	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
168	// PendingEffect
169	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170	
171	/// Internal type alias for the effect setup closure.
172	type EffectSetupFn<M> = Box<
173	    dyn FnOnce(
174	        &<M as Machine>::Context,
175	        &<M as Machine>::Props,
176	        StrongSend<<M as Machine>::Event>,
177	    ) -> CleanupFn,
178	>;
179	
180	/// Typed metadata attached to a pending effect.
181	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
182	pub enum EffectMetadata {
183	    /// Resize an element to fit its content.
184	    ResizeToContent(ResizeToContentEffect),
185	}
186	
187	/// Metadata for an element content-resize effect.
188	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
189	pub struct ResizeToContentEffect {
190	    /// ID of the element to resize.
191	    pub element_id: String,
192	
193	    /// Optional CSS maximum height to apply while resizing.
194	    pub max_height: Option<String>,
195	
196	    /// Optional maximum row count used by adapters that derive a height cap
197	    /// from computed textarea line metrics.
198	    pub max_rows: Option<u32>,
199	}
200	
201	/// A named side effect produced by a state transition.
202	///
203	/// Pending effects are returned from [`Service::send`] inside [`SendResult`].
204	/// The framework adapter is responsible for executing them by calling
205	/// [`run`](PendingEffect::run).
206	///
207	/// The setup function receives context, props, and a strong send handle.
208	/// It returns a [`CleanupFn`] invoked when the effect must stop (state
209	/// change or unmount).

content.text: Confirmed: `type Effect: EffectName` requires **`Copy`**, so `Effect::LoadChildren(Key)` is impossible — I must use the unit-Effect + transient-context-field pattern (same as NavMenu's `requested_focus_id` / `ScrollFocusedIntoView`). The other gating unknown is whether `TreeCollection` supports runtime child insertion (needed for `ChildrenLoaded`). Let me check.

468	    }
469	
470	    fn children_of<'a>(&'a self, parent_key: &Key) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'a Node<T>>
471	    where
472	        T: 'a,
473	    {
474	        // Returns direct children from all_nodes (not just visible ones).
475	        self.all_nodes
476	            .iter()
477	            .filter(move |n| n.parent_key.as_ref() == Some(parent_key))

480	
481	/// Mutation methods used by `MutableTreeData`. These operate on the internal
482	/// flat `all_nodes` vec, `key_to_index` map, and `visible_indices` cache.
483	///
484	/// After structural mutations (insert, remove, reparent), `rebuild_indices()`
485	/// recomputes `key_to_index`, per-node `index` fields, and `visible_indices`.
486	impl<T: CollectionItem> TreeCollection<T> {
487	    /// Insert a child node under `parent` at the given sibling index.
488	    ///
489	    /// `sibling_index` is the position among the parent's direct children
490	    /// (or among root nodes when `parent` is `None`). The node is inserted
491	    /// into `all_nodes` at the correct DFS position and indices are rebuilt.
492	    ///
493	    /// Returns the flat DFS index of the inserted node on success, or
494	    /// `None` if `parent` is `Some(k)` and `k` is not present in the tree
495	    /// (rejected to avoid creating dangling parent references).
496	    pub fn insert_child(
497	        &mut self,
498	        parent: Option<&Key>,
499	        sibling_index: usize,
500	        item: T,
501	    ) -> Option<usize> {
502	        // Reject inserts under a nonexistent parent.
503	        if let Some(pk) = parent
504	            && !self.key_to_index.contains_key(pk)
505	        {
506	            return None;
507	        }
508	
509	        let key = item.key().clone();
510	
511	        let (level, parent_key_owned) = if let Some(pk) = parent {
512	            let parent_level = self.all_nodes[self.key_to_index[pk]].level;
513	
514	            (parent_level + 1, Some(pk.clone()))
515	        } else {
516	            (0, None)
517	        };
518	
519	        let text_value = item.text_value().to_string();
520	
521	        let node = Node {
522	            key: key.clone(),
523	            node_type: NodeType::Item,
524	            value: Some(item),
525	            text_value,
526	            level,
527	            has_children: false,
528	            is_expanded: None,
529	            parent_key: parent_key_owned.clone(),
530	            index: 0, // recomputed by rebuild_indices
531	        };
532	
533	        // Determine flat insertion position: after the parent's existing
534	        // children at `sibling_index`, or among roots.
535	        let flat_pos = self.flat_insert_position(parent_key_owned.as_ref(), sibling_index);
536	
537	        self.all_nodes.insert(flat_pos, node);

This closes a scope gap found by an audit: both variants are fully specified but unimplemented. Follow the workspace conventions strictly (spec-first, zero warnings, missing_docs, `#[expect]` not `#[allow]`, `.expect()` not `.unwrap()`, no_cleanup() for no-op effect cleanup).

Spec: spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md — READ §5 (L1678-1740) and §6 (L1741-1954) end-to-end first.

## Hard architectural constraints (already analyzed — do not deviate)

1. **`Effect` MUST be `Copy`.** `ars_core::Machine::Effect: EffectName` where `EffectName: Copy + Debug + Eq + Hash + Send + Sync + 'static` (verified in crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs:148). So you CANNOT add `Effect::LoadChildren(Key)`.

2. **`LoadChildren` is a typed Effect (machine→adapter), NOT an Event.** The spec §5.2 lists it under "Events", but that predates this workspace's typed-Effect convention (the same staleness the NavigationMenu spec had). Adapter notifications here are Effects (see existing `Effect::{ScrollFocusedIntoView, SelectionChange, ExpandedChange, Reorder}`). Implement lazy-load notification as a unit `Effect::LoadChildren` plus a transient `Context` field carrying the key (mirror NavigationMenu's `requested_focus_id: Option<String>` / the `ScrollFocusedIntoView`-reads-`focused_node` pattern). Suggested: `Context.pending_load: Option<Key>`, set when emitting `Effect::LoadChildren`; the adapter reads it and calls its adapter-registered load callback. Then UPDATE spec §5 to describe `LoadChildren` as a typed `Effect` + `pending_load` context field (not an Event), consistent with the rest of the machine.

3. **These ARE Events (consumer→machine inputs):** `ChildrenLoaded { parent, children }`, `LoadError(Key)` (the spec §5.4 step 6 references LoadError even though §5.2 omits it — add it), `RenameStart(Key)`, `RenameCommit { key, new_name: String }`, `RenameCancel(Key)`.

4. **`ChildrenLoaded` payload:** the spec shows `children: Vec<Node<T>>`, but the real insertion API is `TreeCollection::insert_child(&mut self, parent: Option<&Key>, sibling_index: usize, item: T) -> Option<usize>` (crates/ars-collections/src/tree_collection.rs:496, available when `T: CollectionItem`). VERIFY whether `TreeItem` implements `CollectionItem` (grep ars-collections + tree_view). Choose the cleanest payload type that works with the real collection API (likely `Vec<TreeItemConfig<TreeItem>>` or `Vec<(Key, String, TreeItem)>` — whatever lets you insert children under `parent` and rebuild). Update spec §5.2 to the payload type you actually use. Do NOT invent a `Node<T>` public constructor if one doesn't exist.

## What to implement

**§5 Lazy Loading:**
- `NodeLoadState` enum { Loaded, Loading, Error, NotLoaded } (derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash).
- `Context.load_state: BTreeMap<Key, NodeLoadState>` + `Context.pending_load: Option<Key>`. Seed `load_state` in `init` (a node with the `has_children` affordance but no loaded children = `NotLoaded`; otherwise `Loaded`). Update the `Context` literal in `init` AND in the `SyncProps` rebuild.
- `Effect::LoadChildren` (unit variant).
- Transition: expanding a node whose `load_state == NotLoaded` (via `ExpandNode`/`ToggleNode`/`ExpandAll`) sets `load_state[key]=Loading`, sets `pending_load=Some(key)`, and emits `Effect::LoadChildren` (in addition to the normal expand plan). `ChildrenLoaded{parent, children}` inserts the children under `parent` into `ctx.items` and sets `load_state[parent]=Loaded`. `LoadError(key)` sets `load_state[key]=Error`.
- Api accessors: e.g. `node_load_state(&Key) -> NodeLoadState`, `is_loading(&Key) -> bool`. The existing `loading_label()` already exists — wire branch attrs / a `data-ars-loading` or `aria-busy` where the spec/§5.4 implies (check §2 anatomy + existing branch_attrs for how loading is surfaced; keep it minimal and spec-faithful).

**§6 Renamable:**
- `Props.renamable: bool` (default false) + builder `renamable(bool)`.
- `Context.renaming_key: Option<Key>` (init: None; preserve/clear appropriately in SyncProps).
- `Event::{RenameStart(Key), RenameCommit{key,new_name}, RenameCancel(Key)}`.
- `Part::NodeRenameInput { node_id: Key }` — add to the `Part` enum AND to `part_attrs` match. Add `node_rename_input_attrs(&self, node_id: &Key) -> AttrMap` per spec §6.5 (type=text, value=label, aria-label from `Messages.rename_label`). Add `on_rename_input_keydown`, `on_rename_input_blur`, `is_renaming` per spec §6.5. Wire `F2` in `on_node_keydown_impl` to dispatch `RenameStart(focused)`.
- Transition per spec §6.4: `RenameStart` only from Idle/Focused, ignored if `!renamable` or node disabled; if another node is renaming, commit it first (fire `RenameCommit` for the previous — match the spec's described behavior as closely as the architecture allows) then set `renaming_key=Some(key)` + move focus. `RenameCommit`/`RenameCancel` clear `renaming_key` (guarded to the matching key).
- `Messages.rename_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>` default `|node_name, _| format!("Rename {node_name}")`. Add a type alias like the existing `DragHandleLabelFn`.

**Doc fixes (same file, while you're here):**
- `Event::FocusNext` / `FocusPrev` doc comments wrongly say "wraps to the first/last" — the machine does NOT wrap (see `focus_relative`). Fix both to "(does not wrap)".
- `is_descendant` doc comment overstates ("is `ancestor` itself or sits below") — it returns false for candidate==ancestor. Drop the "itself" claim.

## Tests (TDD — write first, watch fail, then implement)

- Unit tests (inline `#[cfg(test)]`): lazy expand emits `Effect::LoadChildren` + sets Loading + pending_load; ChildrenLoaded inserts + marks Loaded; LoadError marks Error; RenameStart sets renaming_key (guarded by renamable + disabled + state); RenameCommit/Cancel clear it; rename-while-renaming commits previous; F2 dispatches RenameStart; node_rename_input_attrs shape; is_renaming.
- Snapshot tests (insta): `node_rename_input_attrs` output; any new branch loading attr. If you add/modify `.snap` files, that's expected.
- Update spec-conformance test `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/navigation/tree_view.rs` to include `Part::NodeRenameInput` in the expected anatomy list, matching `Part::all()` order (add the variant to the `Part` enum at the position that yields the intended anatomy order, then update the test to match).
- Update proptest arbitraries in `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/navigation/tree_view.rs` to also generate the new events (ChildrenLoaded, LoadError, RenameStart, RenameCommit, RenameCancel) so the invariant proptest exercises them. (Do NOT edit `tests/proptest_state_machines/navigation/mod.rs` — another workstream owns it.)

## Spec sync (tree-view.md §5/§6)
Update §5 and §6 to match what you actually implemented (the typed-Effect LoadChildren model + pending_load, the real ChildrenLoaded payload type, the NodeRenameInput part position, LoadError event). Keep Rust code blocks syntactically valid (they're parsed by `syn` via `spec compile-snippets`) or tag illustrative/partial blocks `rust,no_check`. Run `cargo xtask spec validate` after.

```

cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -20

## Summary

Edited only `spec/components/navigation/navigation-menu.md` to match the shipped implementation at `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/navigation_menu/mod.rs`. No `.rs` files were touched.

### Edits by section

- **§1.2 Events (N4)**: Added `SetItems(Vec<Key>)`, `SyncProps`, `SyncControlledValue(Option<Key>)`, and `SyncMessages { locale, messages }` with docs explaining the registration/sync surface.

- **§1.3 Context (N4)**: Added `items_registered: bool`, `pending_open_item: Option<Key>`, and `requested_focus_id: Option<String>` in the impl's field order, with doc comments.

- **§1.6 Full Machine Implementation (N2, N3, N6)**: Complete rewrite from inline-closure/string-named effects calling `use_platform_effects()`/`set_timeout`/`focus_element_by_id` to the typed `Effect` enum (`OpenDelay`, `CloseDelay`, `FocusTrigger`, `ValueChange`) + `PendingEffect::named(...)` / `cancel_effect(...)`, delegating all DOM/timer work to the adapter. Added `type Effect = Effect`. Rewrote `transition` to the impl's structure (combined `Close`/`SelectLink`, `PointerLeave`/`ContentPointerLeave`; staged `pending_open_item` for the open-delay timer; added all four sync arms). Added the shared transition helpers (`item_is_registered`, `effective_open_item`, `open_item_plan`, `open_to_plan`, `close_plan`, `value_change_effect`, `focus_by_offset_plan`, `focus_absolute_plan`) so the **registration gate (N6)** is documented — open is gated on registered items so an unregistered/stale-controlled key can't open a phantom panel. Added a prose note on the typed-Effect/adapter-timer convention and `on_props_changed`'s sync-event mapping. Fenced as `rust,no_check` per the workspace convention for large machine snippets (matches accordion.md).

- **§1.7 Connect / API**:
  - **N1**: `Part` enum extended from 8 to 15 variants — added `Sub`, `SubList`, `SubItem { item_key }`, `SubTrigger { item_key, content_id }`, `SubContent { item_key }`, `SubIndicator`, `SubViewport`. Routed all 15 through `part_attrs` and added the corresponding `sub_*_attrs` methods.
  - **N3**: Trigger/content DOM ids now use `trigger_dom_id`/`content_dom_id` via `dom_safe_key_token` (e.g. `nav-trigger-s-<hex>`) instead of raw `item_key.to_string()` / `ids.item("content", item_key)`. Added the `item_is_registered`, `trigger_dom_id`, `content_dom_id`, and `dom_safe_key_token` free functions. `open_item`/`is_item_open` now gated on registration; `indicator_attrs`/`viewport_attrs` use `open_item().is_some()`.
  - **N5**: `motion_direction` now returns `None` on the equal-index tie (added the `else if curr_idx < prev_idx` / `else None` branch); fixed the doc that wrongly mentioned `to-start`/`to-end`.
  - **N7**: `on_trigger_keydown` and `on_content_keydown` now take `now_ms: u64` and forward it to `EscapeKey` instead of calling `use_platform_effects().now_ms()`. Also collapsed the redundant horizontal ArrowUp/ArrowDown branches and corrected the LTR/Auto arm collapse to match the impl. Extracted the roving-tabindex logic into a `trigger_tab_index` helper (shared by root and sub triggers).

Edited only `spec/components/navigation/navigation-menu.md` to match the shipped implementation at `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/navigation_menu/mod.rs`. No `.rs` files were touched.

### Edits by section

- **§1.2 Events (N4)**: Added `SetItems(Vec<Key>)`, `SyncProps`, `SyncControlledValue(Option<Key>)`, and `SyncMessages { locale, messages }` with docs explaining the registration/sync surface.

- **§1.3 Context (N4)**: Added `items_registered: bool`, `pending_open_item: Option<Key>`, and `requested_focus_id: Option<String>` in the impl's field order, with doc comments.

- **§1.6 Full Machine Implementation (N2, N3, N6)**: Complete rewrite from inline-closure/string-named effects calling `use_platform_effects()`/`set_timeout`/`focus_element_by_id` to the typed `Effect` enum (`OpenDelay`, `CloseDelay`, `FocusTrigger`, `ValueChange`) + `PendingEffect::named(...)` / `cancel_effect(...)`, delegating all DOM/timer work to the adapter. Added `type Effect = Effect`. Rewrote `transition` to the impl's structure (combined `Close`/`SelectLink`, `PointerLeave`/`ContentPointerLeave`; staged `pending_open_item` for the open-delay timer; added all four sync arms). Added the shared transition helpers (`item_is_registered`, `effective_open_item`, `open_item_plan`, `open_to_plan`, `close_plan`, `value_change_effect`, `focus_by_offset_plan`, `focus_absolute_plan`) so the **registration gate (N6)** is documented — open is gated on registered items so an unregistered/stale-controlled key can't open a phantom panel. Added a prose note on the typed-Effect/adapter-timer convention and `on_props_changed`'s sync-event mapping. Fenced as `rust,no_check` per the workspace convention for large machine snippets (matches accordion.md).

- **§1.7 Connect / API**:
  - **N1**: `Part` enum extended from 8 to 15 variants — added `Sub`, `SubList`, `SubItem { item_key }`, `SubTrigger { item_key, content_id }`, `SubContent { item_key }`, `SubIndicator`, `SubViewport`. Routed all 15 through `part_attrs` and added the corresponding `sub_*_attrs` methods.
  - **N3**: Trigger/content DOM ids now use `trigger_dom_id`/`content_dom_id` via `dom_safe_key_token` (e.g. `nav-trigger-s-<hex>`) instead of raw `item_key.to_string()` / `ids.item("content", item_key)`. Added the `item_is_registered`, `trigger_dom_id`, `content_dom_id`, and `dom_safe_key_token` free functions. `open_item`/`is_item_open` now gated on registration; `indicator_attrs`/`viewport_attrs` use `open_item().is_some()`.
  - **N5**: `motion_direction` now returns `None` on the equal-index tie (added the `else if curr_idx < prev_idx` / `else None` branch); fixed the doc that wrongly mentioned `to-start`/`to-end`.
  - **N7**: `on_trigger_keydown` and `on_content_keydown` now take `now_ms: u64` and forward it to `EscapeKey` instead of calling `use_platform_effects().now_ms()`. Also collapsed the redundant horizontal ArrowUp/ArrowDown branches and corrected the LTR/Auto arm collapse to match the impl. Extracted the roving-tabindex logic into a `trigger_tab_index` helper (shared by root and sub triggers).

### Validation output

- `cargo xtask spec validate` → `Validated 340 files (components + adapters). All checks passed.`
- `cargo xtask spec lint-code` → **0 findings for navigation-menu.md**. The run reports 201 findings total, but all are pre-existing across unrelated files (breadcrumbs, heading, highlight, keyboard, landmark, etc.) and were not introduced by these edits — navigation-menu.md produces a clean lint.</result>

32	            text_value: "Alpha".to_string(),

41	                },

44	                    text_value: "Gamma".to_string(),
45	                    value: tv_item("Gamma"),
46	                    children: Vec::new(),
47	                    default_expanded: false,
48	                },
49	            ],
50	            default_expanded: false,
51	        },
52	        TreeItemConfig {
53	            key: Key::int(4),
54	            text_value: "Delta".to_string(),
55	            value: tv_item("Delta"),
56	            children: Vec::new(),
57	            default_expanded: false,
58	        },
59	    ])
60	}

88	fn arb_tree_view_props() -> impl Strategy<Value = tree_view::Props> {
89	    (tv_mode(), any::<bool>(), any::<bool>(), any::<bool>()).prop_map(
90	        |(mode, multiple, dnd, expand_alpha)| {
91	            let mut expanded = BTreeSet::new();
92	
93	            if expand_alpha {

96	
97	            tree_view::Props::new()

101	                .multiple(multiple)

103	                .default_expanded(expanded)

105	    )
106	}
107	
108	fn arb_tree_view_event() -> impl Strategy<Value = tree_view::Event> {
109	    prop_oneof![

167	fn tv_is_descendant(
168	    items: &TreeCollection<tree_view::TreeItem>,
169	    ancestor: &Key,
170	    candidate: &Key,
171	) -> bool {
172	    let mut current = items
173	        .get(candidate)
174	        .and_then(|node| node.parent_key.clone());
175	
176	    while let Some(parent) = current {
177	        if &parent == ancestor {
178	            return true;
179	        }
180	
181	        current = items.get(&parent).and_then(|node| node.parent_key.clone());
182	    }
183	
184	    false
185	}

content.text:
All helper names referenced by the accordion and navmenu spec rewrites exist in the impl (no fabrication), and every spec-sync agent reported `spec validate` + snippet-compile passing. Good fidelity.

Here's where things stand. I'll pause for the TreeView agent (the one remaining background task) since the full compile/test pass depends on it.

**Done so far:**
- **Part B (test reorg):** both monolithic files split into per-component modules mirroring `input/`; verified — all 8 spec-conformance tests pass, all moved proptests run green.
- **Phase 1 spec syncs (6 files):** tabs, accordion, pagination, navigation-menu, link, steps all synced to the shipped impl by parallel agents — each reports `cargo xtask spec validate` + snippet-compile passing, and I cross-checked that the rewrites reference **real** impl symbols (no fabrication).
- **Steps S1:** impl now marks the last step `Complete` on `NextStep` (your decision); test updated.
- **Breadcrumbs B1:** placeholder nit fixed.
- **Part C proptests:** new `link.rs` (focus/press invariants + Navigate-effect gating) and `navigation_menu.rs` (Idle↔Open invariants, registration gate, trigger attr shape) written and registered in `mod.rs`.

**In flight (background):**
- **TreeView §5 Lazy Loading + §6 Renamable** variant implementation (your "implement both now" decision) — the agent has already added `Part::NodeRenameInput`, the `ChildrenLoaded`/`LoadError`/`Rename*` events (with `Vec<TreeItemConfig<TreeItem>>` payload), and updated the conformance + proptest files. It's still finishing the impl + tests + spec sync.

Once it completes I'll run the unified compile/clippy/test pass, do Phase 2 (iterative "anything missing?") and Phase 3 (coverage), then the full `cargo xci-fast` gate before presenting for your review. I'll be notified when the agent finishes.

<summary>Agent "Implement TreeView lazy-load + renamable variants" completed</summary>
<result>The only clippy/compile error across all targets is the pre-existing `navigation_menu.rs` `Locale` error (a parallel workstream's incomplete file). Everything in my scope — lib, inline tests, spec_conformance — is clean.

The post-implementation audit is complete. Let me give the consolidated summary.

## Post-implementation audit summary

**Phase 1 (spec/impl drift):** Landed spec edits so the §1 canonical contract blocks include all variant additions (matching the convention where §4 DnD parts/effects/props live in §1): `Part::NodeRenameInput` (in `Part` enum + `part_attrs`), `Effect::LoadChildren`, the 5 new `Event`s, `Context::{load_state, pending_load, renaming_key}`, `Props::renamable`, `Messages::rename_label`; updated §2 anatomy tree+table (NodeRenameInput row, `aria-busy`/`data-ars-loading`), §1.3 init NOTE, and rewrote §5/§6 to the typed-Effect+`pending_load` model with the real `ChildrenLoaded` payload and `LoadError` event.

**Phase 2 (iterative "anything missing"):** Round 1 fixed adapter-spec drift (`tree_view::LoadChildren`→`LoadChildrenFn`, added NodeRenameInput part-mapping rows in both Leptos/Dioxus specs). **Round 2 found a real bug:** the lazy-load detection used a set-diff that silently failed to retry an already-expanded-but-`NotLoaded` branch — refactored `expanded_change_plan` to take an explicit `expanding: &[Key]` list. Added 8 edge-case tests. Rounds 3-5 added the events-summary-table rows and confirmed two clean rounds (parity table, adapter prelude/feature-wiring out of scope — no adapter exists yet).

**Phase 3 (coverage):** llvm-cov surfaced two uncovered new branches — the SyncProps "clear renaming_key when node removed" path and `part_attrs(Part::NodeRenameInput)`. Added 3 tests. tree_view/mod.rs coverage: regions 97.66%→98.12%, lines 98.24%→98.59%. All remaining uncovered lines are pre-existing (DnD guards, `on_node_keydown_at`, `clamp_focus` ancestor-walk) — none are my new code.

**Verification:** lib tests 174 (tree_view) / 3929 (full) pass; spec_conformance pass; clippy lib+spec_conformance clean; docs no tree_view warnings; snapshot orphan check clean (`--features i18n`); spec validate + compile-snippets + xtask corpus all green. Only failure anywhere is the pre-existing `navigation_menu.rs` `Locale` error in a parallel workstream's file (not mine).

Let me give the final report.

---

## Report

I implemented the TreeView §5 (Lazy Loading) and §6 (Renamable Nodes) variants in the agnostic core via TDD, then ran the full three-phase post-implementation audit. All work is in this one change set.

### New public API surface (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tree_view/mod.rs`)

**§5 Lazy Loading:**
- `pub enum NodeLoadState { Loaded, Loading, Error, NotLoaded }` — derives `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`.
- `pub type LoadChildrenFn = alloc::rc::Rc<dyn Fn(Key)>` (adapter callback alias).
- `Context.load_state: BTreeMap<Key, NodeLoadState>` + `Context.pending_load: Option<Key>`.
- `Effect::LoadChildren` (unit variant).
- `Event::ChildrenLoaded { parent: Key, children: Vec<TreeItemConfig<TreeItem>> }`, `Event::LoadError(Key)`.
- `Api::node_load_state(&Key) -> NodeLoadState`, `Api::is_loading(&Key) -> bool`. Loading branches now emit `aria-busy="true"` + `data-ars-loading` (via `apply_node_state`).

**§6 Renamable:**
- `Props.renamable: bool` (default false) + `renamable(bool)` builder.
- `Context.renaming_key: Option<Key>`.
- `Event::{RenameStart(Key), RenameCommit { key, new_name: String }, RenameCancel(Key)}`.
- `Part::NodeRenameInput { node_id: Key }` (declared between `LeafText` and `DragHandle`).
- `Api::{node_rename_input_attrs(&Key) -> AttrMap, on_rename_input_keydown(&Key, &str, &str), on_rename_input_blur(&Key, &str), is_renaming(&Key) -> bool}`.
- `Messages.rename_label: MessageFn<RenameLabelFn>` (default `|node_name, _| "Rename {node_name}"`) + `pub type RenameLabelFn`.
- F2 in `on_node_keydown_impl` dispatches `RenameStart(focused)`.
- Doc fixes: `FocusNext`/`FocusPrev` now say "(does not wrap)"; `is_descendant` doc no longer claims "itself".

### LoadChildren effect / pending_load design as built
`Effect::LoadChildren` is a unit `Copy` effect (the `EffectName: Copy` constraint forbids `LoadChildren(Key)`). The key travels in the transient `Context.pending_load`, set alongside `load_state[key]=Loading` when an expanding branch is `NotLoaded`. Implemented inside `expanded_change_plan`, which now takes an explicit `expanding: &[Key]` list (the keys the event expands) — fires for the first `NotLoaded` key, works for `ExpandNode`/`ToggleNode`/`ExpandAll`, fires for both controlled and uncontrolled, and retries on re-expand of a still-`NotLoaded` branch.

### ChildrenLoaded payload choice
`Vec<TreeItemConfig<TreeItem>>`. `TreeItem` does **not** implement `CollectionItem` (it has no key field — keys live on `Node`/`TreeItemConfig`), so the real `insert_child` (which requires `T: CollectionItem`) is unusable, and there is no public `Node<T>` constructor. `TreeItemConfig` is the public construction type for `TreeCollection` and carries key/text/value/children/default_expanded. I splice the loaded configs under `parent` and rebuild via `TreeCollection::new` using only the `T: Clone` public API (`all_nodes`/`children_of`/`is_expanded`) in a new private `insert_loaded_children` helper. This required adding `#[derive(Clone, PartialEq)]` to `TreeItemConfig` in `crates/ars-collections/src/tree_collection.rs:20` (conditional on `T`; the manual `Debug` is preserved) so it can ride in the `Clone + PartialEq` `Event` enum.

### Tests added (`tree_view/tests.rs`, 24 new; 174 total pass)
Lazy: init seeding, lazy/loaded/toggle/expand-all triggering, ChildrenLoaded insert+Loaded, LoadError, SyncProps reseed, re-expand retry, controlled-expand still loads, error stickiness, aria-busy, unknown-parent/node ignored, `part_attrs` dispatch. Rename: default false, init None, RenameStart guards (renamable/disabled/unknown/state), commit/cancel clear (guarded), rename-while-renaming retarget, F2 dispatch, input attrs shape + snapshot, keydown Enter/Escape, blur commit/noop, is_renaming, SyncProps clears/keeps renaming_key on node removal.

### Spec edits (`spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md`)
Rewrote §5/§6 to the typed-Effect+`pending_load` model, real `ChildrenLoaded` payload, `LoadError` event, retarget-rename behavior; promoted all variant additions into the §1 canonical `Part`/`Event`/`Effect`/`Context`/`Props`/`Messages` blocks (matching the §4 DnD convention); updated §1.2 events table, §1.3 init NOTE, §2 anatomy tree+table. Also corrected `LoadChildren`→`LoadChildrenFn` and added `NodeRenameInput` part-mapping rows in `spec/leptos-components/navigation/tree-view.md` and `spec/dioxus-components/navigation/tree-view.md`. Spec-conformance test (`tests/spec_conformance/navigation/tree_view.rs`) and proptest arbitraries (`tests/proptest_state_machines/navigation/tree_view.rs`) updated.

### .snap files touched
One new: `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tree_view/snapshots/ars_components__navigation__tree_view__tests__tree_view_node_rename_input.snap` (accepted via `INSTA_UPDATE=always`, re-run green). **This PR creates a `.snap` fixture, so the `snapshot-reviewed` label should be applied when the PR is opened.**

### Verification results
- `cargo test -p ars-components --lib navigation::tree_view` — 174 passed
- `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance navigation::tree_view` — 1 passed
- `cargo clippy -p ars-components --lib --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean (also clean on `--test spec_conformance`)
- `cargo xtask spec validate` — 340 files, all checks passed
- `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` — 2174 blocks, no findings; `cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets` passed
- `cargo doc -p ars-components --all-features` — zero tree_view warnings
- `cargo insta test -p ars-components --lib --features i18n --unreferenced=reject` — no unreferenced snapshots
- Coverage: tree_view/mod.rs regions 97.66%→98.12%, lines 98.24%→98.59%; all remaining uncovered lines are pre-existing code, none new.

### Judgment calls / flags
1. **Proptest binary cannot fully compile** due to a pre-existing error in a sibling file, `tests/proptest_state_machines/navigation/navigation_menu.rs:201` (`_locale_import_marker(_: Locale)` — `Locale` not imported), owned by a parallel navigation-refactor workstream (the worktree had substantial uncommitted in-flight work despite the clean status snapshot). I verified my `tree_view.rs` proptest changes compile by a temporary, fully-reverted local patch — the binary builds with my changes once that unrelated line is fixed. I did not touch `navigation_menu.rs` or the off-limits `navigation/mod.rs`.
2. **Commit-previous-rename behavior:** spec §6.4 says starting a rename while another is active "commits the previous with its current input value." The agnostic core can't read the live DOM value, so the machine just retargets `renaming_key`; the adapter's `on_rename_input_blur` fires `RenameCommit` for the outgoing input as focus moves. I documented this in code and updated the spec prose/transition example accordingly.
3. **`load_state` reseed on SyncProps** only when `items_changed` (preserves in-flight `Loading`/`Error` across unrelated prop echoes); `default_expanded` lazy branches stay `NotLoaded` at init (load is user-driven), with re-expand retrying.
4. The pre-existing MD060 table-alignment lint warnings and other-component doc-link warnings are not mine and were left untouched.

No commit/push performed (awaiting your review per workflow).</result>
